pattern: Because swapping backward is always free, paying to reach any position at or before the target and then drifting forward for free is never worse than paying directly, so the cheapest way to reach position i is simply the smallest paid swap seen anywhere from the start up through i.
hints: Swapping with someone behind you is free, so once you've paid to reach some position j, you can always drift forward to any position past j at no extra cost.

That means the cheapest way to reach position i is to pay for the single cheapest swap among positions 0 through i, and then coast forward for free if needed -- you never need more than one paid swap.
Scan left to right keeping a running minimum of the costs seen so far; that running minimum at index i is the answer for position i.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a dip in the middle followed by a rise
statement example, cheapest swap sits right at the front
strictly decreasing costs, so each new position sets a new cheaper running minimum
strictly increasing costs, so the very first swap is always the cheapest for everything after it

stress.description: random costs across the full allowed length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(100, size))
    cost = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [cost]
